subject: Sports
title: Jahangir Khan: 555 Consecutive Wins
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Jahangir Khan: 555 Consecutive Wins.

hook.text: Five hundred and fifty-five matches without a single loss. The longest winning streak in any professional sport.

buildup.text: Pakistani squash player Jahangir Khan went unbeaten from 1981 to 1986—over five years of total dominance.

discovery.text: He won every tournament he entered for five straight years. Opponents knew they were playing for second place.

twist.text: The streak ended when Ross Norman beat him in the 1986 World Open final. Khan was just 22 years old.

climax.text: 555 consecutive wins across five years. No athlete in any sport has come close to this consistency.

punchline.text: Five years without losing. The longest streak in all of professional sport.

quiz.question: How long did Jahangir Khan's winning streak last?
